steps.title: Identify the logic
steps.content: The first sentence explains that residents rely on the Lanzhou Metro for millions of trips each year. The second sentence says city officials work to maintain the system's stations so those trips are smooth. Heavy use gives officials a reason to maintain the system.

steps.title: Remove wrong signals
steps.content: Choice B would introduce an alternative, but station maintenance is not an alternative to metro ridership. Choice C would introduce an example, but the maintenance effort is not an example of millions of trips. Choice D would introduce contrast, though the two sentences are consistent.

steps.title: Choose the consequence
steps.content: The transition should show that the maintenance follows logically from the metro's heavy use. Accordingly signals that the second sentence is a natural consequence of the first sentence's information about many annual trips. The answer is A.
